# Generated by ContextEngine — npx @strifero/contextengine
# https://github.com/strifero/ContextEngine

# Project Context

## Stack
- Next.js 14 (App Router)
- TypeScript (strict mode)
- Prisma ORM → PostgreSQL
- Tailwind CSS
- ESLint + Prettier

## Architecture
- `app/` — Next.js routes and layouts (App Router)
- `components/` — Shared UI components
- `lib/` — Server-side utilities and service clients
- `prisma/` — Database schema and migrations
- `public/` — Static assets

## Conventions

### Routing
- All routes go under `app/` using the App Router file convention
- Page components live in `app/<route>/page.tsx`
- Layout components live in `app/<route>/layout.tsx`
- API routes are Route Handlers at `app/api/<route>/route.ts`

### Components
- Server Components by default — only add `"use client"` when you need interactivity, browser APIs, or hooks
- Never import server-only modules (database, secrets) into Client Components
- Shared UI components in `components/` should be client-safe unless explicitly marked

### Database
- Database access only in Server Components, Route Handlers, or Server Actions — never in Client Components
- Prisma client is a singleton — import from `lib/prisma.ts`, never instantiate directly
- Use `prisma.$transaction()` for operations that must be atomic
- Prefer `include` over separate queries for related data

### Validation
- Use `zod` for all runtime validation at API and form boundaries
- Define schemas alongside the code that uses them

### TypeScript
- Strict mode is enabled — no implicit any
- Prefer `type` over `interface` for object shapes
- Use `satisfies` operator for type-safe object literals

## What to avoid
- Do not use `getServerSideProps` or `getStaticProps` — this project uses the App Router exclusively
- Do not use the `pages/` directory — it is not part of this project
- Do not create Prisma client instances outside `lib/prisma.ts`
- Do not use `any` — use `unknown` and narrow the type
